titleStmt.title: Letter from Marie Martin to her mother, Mary Martin, 12 November 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Marie Martin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A short letter from Marie Martin (1892-1975) to her mother Mary. Marie writes to reassure her mother that she is feeling fine and is back on duty in the hospital. She mentions that a parcel she is waiting for hasn't arrived yet.Marie Helena Martin, later known as 'Mother Mary', was a volunteer nurse during the First World War. She served in field hospitals in Malta, Leeds, and France working for the British army and later went on to become the founder of the Medical Missionaries of Mary (MMM).

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
12th Nov 1915 My dearest Mother, This is just a short
note to say I am A 1 & in the
best of form, on duty again &
as happy as a lark with plenty
of work . Ever so many thanks
for your sweet letter, it was
very amusing about Mr Gravely
writing you two letters, do enclose
them in one of yours. I should
love to see them. I only spoke
to him about twice & he seemed
a dear Old Man & very anxious
to do anything for me, he was
pretty amused when I told him
about the Castle hospital & the
officer. he never mention to
me he was going to write
to you, get U B & give him
dinner or something he loves
dear dirty Dublin & says they
always got a great reception
when the Oxfordshire goes there.
The parcel has not arrived yet
but takes about 3 weeks to come
Shall end this & will write long
letter tomorrow. With love in haste Marie
